title: peucedanum officinale subsp. paniculatum
intro: Peucedanum officinale subsp. paniculatum, commonly known as garden angelica or wild angelica, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the carrot family. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties. The health benefits of Peucedanum officinale subsp. paniculatum include its ability to relieve respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, as well as its potential to aid in digestion and reduce menstrual cramps. The herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat various health ailments and promote overall well-being.
